author: claude
content: [independent re-validation] Picked this up fresh from master (unaware the fix already existed) and reproduced + re-validated. Pre-fix the 4 Hypothesis liveness tests reproduce the bug; minimal counterexample for the transient case: plan=[(1,1,"success")], fail_writes=1 -- one visit, one record, finalize(success), first write_table raises -> visit never reaches the completion queue, batch left in _batches, per-finalize flush_events never fire so the token never resolves, and the RuntimeError escapes shutdown before the completion-queue puts run. Post-fix (branch fix/storage-forward-progress): all 4 invariants green (4 passed in 4.55s, hypothesis 6.155.6). Confirmed the atomic flush_cache + bounded shutdown retry + survive-transient-error in save_batch_if_past_timeout correctly upholds I1-I4 -- fix is correct and complete. Independently rediscovered the documented contract boundary (zero-record never-finalized visit is invisible to the controller -> WQ client responsibility) and the multiprocess.Queue feeder-thread lag (item not visible to empty()/get() immediately after put(); production unaffected via repeated get_new_completed_visits polling). Linked #28/#30: SAME bug class -- a structured-storage write error that should be loud instead causes silent data loss. Here it escaped and aborted the drain (stranded visit); in #28/#30 the SQLite provider catches and silently drops the record. Same fix family: surface the error, guarantee a terminal state, never swallow or strand. #28/#30 remain to be addressed in the SQLite/SQLAlchemy provider.
